Matthew1:1 This is the scroll of the genealogy of Jesus, the Anointed One, the son of David and descendant of Abraham." 
Matthew1:2 Abraham had a son named Isaac, who had a son named Jacob, who had a son named Judah (he and his brothers became the tribes of Israel)." 
Matthew1:3 Judah and Tamar had twin sons, Perez and Zerah. Perez had a son named Hezron, who had a son named Ram," 
Matthew1:4 who had a son named Amminadab, who had a son named Nashon," 
Matthew1:5 who had a son named Salmon, who, along with Rahab, had a son named Boaz. Boaz and Ruth had a son named Obed, who was the father of Jesse," 
Matthew1:6 and Jesse had a son named David, who became the king." 
Matthew1:7 Then David and Bathsheba had a son named Solomon, who had a son named Rehoboam, who had a son named Abijah, who had a son named Asa," 
Matthew1:8 who had a son named Jehoshaphat, who had a son named Joram, who had a son named Uzziah," 
Matthew1:9 who had a son named Jotham, who had a son named Ahaz, who had a son named Hezekiah," 
Matthew1:10 who had a son named Manasseh, who had a son named Amos, who had a son named Josiah," 
Matthew1:11 who was the father of Jeconiah. It was during the days of Jeconiah and his brothers that Israel was taken captive and deported to Babylon. 
Matthew1:12 About the time of their captivity in Babylon, Jeconiah had a son named Shealtiel, who had a son named Zerubbabel," 
Matthew1:13 who had a son named Abiud, who had a son named Eliakim," 
Matthew1:14 who had a son named Azor, who had a son named Zadok, who had a son named Achim, who had a son named Eliud," 
Matthew1:15 who had a son named Eleazar, who had a son named Matthan, who had a son named Jacob," 
Matthew1:16 who was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary the mother of Jesus, who is called “the Anointed One.”" 
Matthew1:17 So from Abraham to David were fourteen generations, and from David to the Babylonian captivity, fourteen generations, and from the Babylonian captivity to Christ, fourteen generations." 
Matthew1:18 This is how the birth of Jesus Christ took place. His mother, Mary, had promised Joseph to be his wife, but while she was still a virgin she became pregnant through the power of the Holy Spirit." 
Matthew1:19 Her fiancé, Joseph, was a righteous man full of integrity and he didn’t want to disgrace her, but when he learned of her pregnancy he secretly planned to break the engagement." 
Matthew1:20 While he was still debating with himself about what to do, he fell asleep and had a supernatural dream. An angel from the Lord appeared to him and said, “Joseph, descendant of David, don’t hesitate to take Mary into your home as your wife, because the power of the Holy Spirit has conceived a child in her womb." 
Matthew1:21 She will give birth to a son and you are to name him ‘Savior,’ for he is destined to give his life to save his people from their sins.”" 
Matthew1:22 This happened to fulfill what the Lord had spoken through his prophet: 
Matthew1:23 Listen! A virgin will be pregnant, she will give birth to a Son, and he will be known as “Emmanuel,” which means in Hebrew, “God became one of us.”" 
Matthew1:24 When Joseph woke from his dream, he did all that the angel of the Lord instructed him to do. He took Mary to be his wife," 
Matthew1:25 but they refrained from having sex until she gave birth to her firstborn son, whom they named “Jesus.”" 
Matthew2:1 Jesus was born in Bethlehem near Jerusalem during the reign of King Herod. After Jesus’ birth a group of spiritual priests from the East came to Jerusalem 
Matthew2:2 and inquired of the people, “Where is the child who is born king of the Jewish people? We observed his star rising in the sky and we’ve come to bow before him in worship.”" 
Matthew2:3 King Herod was shaken to the core when he heard this, and not only he, but all of Jerusalem was disturbed when they heard this news." 
Matthew2:4 So he called a meeting of the Jewish ruling priests and religious scholars, demanding that they tell him where the promised Messiah was prophesied to be born." 
Matthew2:5 “He will be born in Bethlehem, in the land of Judah,” they told him. “Because the prophecy states:" 
Matthew2:6 And you, little Bethlehem, are not insignificant among the clans of Judah, for out of you will emerge the Shepherd-King of my people Israel!”" 
Matthew2:7 Then Herod secretly summoned the spiritual priests from the East to ascertain the exact time the star first appeared. 
Matthew2:8 And he told them, “Now go to Bethlehem and carefully look there for the child, and when you’ve found him, report to me so that I can go and bow down and worship him too.”" 
Matthew2:9 And on their way to Bethlehem, the same star they had seen in the East suddenly reappeared! Amazed, they watched as it went ahead of them and stopped directly over the place where the child was." 
Matthew2:10 And when they saw the star, they were so ecstatic that they shouted and celebrated with unrestrained joy." 
Matthew2:11 When they came into the house and saw the young child with Mary, his mother, they fell to the ground at his feet and worshiped him. Then they opened their treasure chests full of gifts and presented him with gold, frankincense, and myrrh." 
Matthew2:12 Afterward they returned to their own country by another route because God had warned them in a dream not to go back to Herod. 
Matthew2:13 After they had gone, Joseph had another dream. An angel of the Lord appeared to him and said, “Get up now and flee to Egypt. Take Mary and the little child and stay there until I tell you to leave, for Herod intends to search for the child to kill him.”" 
Matthew2:14 So that very night he got up and took Jesus and his mother and made their escape to Egypt 
Matthew2:15 and remained there until Herod died. All of this fulfilled what the Lord had spoken through his prophet: I summon my Son out of Egypt. 
Matthew2:16 When Herod realized that he had been tricked by the wise men, he was infuriated. So he sent soldiers with orders to slaughter every baby boy two years old and younger in Bethlehem and throughout the surrounding countryside, based on the time frame he was given from interrogating the wise men." 
Matthew2:17 This fulfilled the words of the prophet Jeremiah: 
Matthew2:18 I hear the screams of anguish, weeping, and wailing in Ramah. Rachel is weeping uncontrollably for her children. And she refuses to be comforted, because they are dead and gone." 
Matthew2:19 After Herod died, an angel of the Lord appeared again to Joseph in a dream while he was still in Egypt," 
Matthew2:20 saying, “Go back to the land of Israel and take the child and his mother with you, for those who sought to kill the child are dead.”" 
Matthew2:21 So he awoke and took Jesus and Mary and returned to the land of Israel. 
Matthew2:22 But when he heard that Archelaus, Herod’s son, had succeeded him as ruler over all of the territory of Judah, he was afraid to go back. Then he had another dream from God, warning him to avoid that region and instructing him instead to go to the province of Galilee." 
Matthew2:23 So he settled his family in the village of Nazareth, fulfilling the prophecy that he would be known as the “Branch.”"

Matthew3:1 It was at this time that John the Baptizer began to preach in the desert of Judah. His message was this: 
Matthew3:2 “Heaven’s kingdom is about to appear—so you’d better keep turning away from evil and turn back to God!” 
Matthew3:3 Isaiah was referring to John when he prophesied: A thunderous voice! One will be crying out in the wilderness, “Prepare yourself for the Lord’s coming and level a straight path inside your hearts for him.”" 
Matthew3:3 Isaiah was referring to John when he prophesied: A thunderous voice! One will be crying out in the wilderness, “Prepare yourself for the Lord’s coming and level a straight path inside your hearts for him.”" 
Matthew3:4 Now, John wore clothing made from camel’s hair, tied at his waist with a leather strap, and his food consisted of dried locusts and wild honey." 
Matthew3:4 Now, John wore clothing made from camel’s hair, tied at his waist with a leather strap, and his food consisted of dried locusts and wild honey." 
Matthew3:5 A steady stream of people from Jerusalem, all the surrounding countryside, and the region near the Jordan came out to the wilderness to be baptized by him." 
Matthew3:6 And while they were publicly confessing their sins, he would immerse them in the Jordan River." 
Matthew3:7 But when he saw many coming from among the wealthy elite of Jewish society and many of the religious leaders known as Pharisees coming to witness the baptism, he began to denounce them, saying, “You offspring of vipers! Who warned you to slither away like snakes from the fire of God’s judgment?" 
Matthew3:8 You must prove your repentance by a changed life. 
Matthew3:9 And don’t presume you can get away with merely saying to yourselves, ‘But we’re Abraham’s descendants!’ For I tell you, God can awaken these stones to become sons of Abraham!" 
Matthew3:10 The axe is now ready to cut down the trees at their very roots. Every fruitless, rotten tree will be chopped down and thrown into the fire." 
Matthew3:11 Those who repent I baptize with water, but there is coming a man after me who is more powerful than I. In fact, I’m not even worthy enough to pick up his sandals. He will submerge you into union with the Spirit of Holiness and with a raging fire!" 
Matthew3:12 He comes with a winnowing fork in his hands and comes to his threshing floor to sift what is worthless from what is pure. And he is ready to sweep out his threshing floor and gather his wheat into his granary, but the straw he will burn up with a fire that can’t be extinguished!”" 
Matthew3:13 Then Jesus left Galilee to come to the Jordan to be baptized by John. 
Matthew3:13 Then Jesus left Galilee to come to the Jordan to be baptized by John. 
Matthew3:14 But when he waded into the water, John resisted him, saying, “Why are you doing this? I’m the one who needs to be baptized by you, and yet you come to be baptized by me?”" 
Matthew3:14 But when he waded into the water, John resisted him, saying, “Why are you doing this? I’m the one who needs to be baptized by you, and yet you come to be baptized by me?”" 
Matthew3:15 Jesus replied, “It is only right to do all that God requires.” Then John baptized Jesus." 
Matthew3:15 Jesus replied, “It is only right to do all that God requires.” Then John baptized Jesus." 
Matthew3:16 And as Jesus rose up out of the water, the heavenly realm opened up over him and he saw the Holy Spirit descend out of the heavens and rest upon him in the form of a dove." 
Matthew3:17 Then suddenly the voice of the Father shouted from the sky, saying, “This is my Son—the Beloved! My greatest delight is in him.”" 
Matthew4:1 Afterward, the Holy Spirit led Jesus into the wilderness to experience the ordeal of testing by the accuser." 
Matthew4:2 And after fasting for forty days, Jesus was extremely hungry." 
Matthew4:3 Then the tempter came to him and said, “How can you possibly be the Son of God and go hungry? Just command these stones to be turned into loaves of bread.”" 
Matthew4:4 He answered, “The Scriptures say: Bread alone will not satisfy, but true life is found in every word that constantly goes forth from God’s mouth.”" 
Matthew4:5 Then the accuser transported Jesus to the holy city of Jerusalem and perched him at the highest point of the temple 
Matthew4:6 and said to him, “If you’re really God’s Son, jump, and the angels will catch you. For it is written in the Scriptures: He will command his angels to protect you and they will lift you up so that you won’t even bruise your foot on a rock.”" 
Matthew4:7 Once again Jesus said to him, “The Scriptures say: You must never put the Lord your God to a test.”" 
Matthew4:8 And the third time the accuser lifted Jesus up onto a very high mountain range and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and all the splendor that goes with it. 
Matthew4:9 “All of these kingdoms I will give to you,” the accuser said, “if only you will kneel down before me and worship me.”" 
Matthew4:10 But Jesus said, “Go away, Satan! For the Scriptures say: Kneel before the Lord your God and worship only him.”" 
Matthew4:11 At once the accuser left him, and angels suddenly gathered around Jesus to minister to his needs." 
Matthew4:12 When Jesus heard that John the Baptizer had been thrown into prison, he went back into Galilee." 
Matthew4:12 When Jesus heard that John the Baptizer had been thrown into prison, he went back into Galilee." 
Matthew4:13 Jesus moved from Nazareth to make his home in Capernaum, which is by Lake Galilee in the land of Zebulun and Naphtali." 
Matthew4:14 He did this to make the prophecy of Isaiah come true: 
Matthew4:15 Listen, you who live in the land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, along the road to the sea and on the other side of the Jordan, and Galilee—the land of the Gentiles!" 
Matthew4:16 You who spend your days shrouded in darkness can now say, “We have seen a brilliant Light.” And those who live in the dark shadow land of death can now say, “The Dawning Light arises on us.”" 
Matthew4:17 From that time on Jesus began to proclaim his message with these words: “Keep turning away from your sins and come back to God, for heaven’s kingdom realm is now accessible.”" 
Matthew4:18 As he was walking by the shore of Lake Galilee, Jesus noticed two fishermen who were brothers. One was nicknamed Keefa (later called Peter), and the other was Andrew, his brother. Watching as they were casting their nets into the water," 
Matthew4:19 Jesus called out to them and said, “Come and follow me, and I will transform you into men who catch people for God.”" 
Matthew4:20 Immediately they dropped their nets and left everything behind to follow Jesus. 
Matthew4:21 Leaving there, Jesus found three other men sitting in a boat, mending their nets. Two were brothers, Jacob and John, and they were with their father, Zebedee. Jesus called Jacob and John to his side and said to them, “Come and follow me.”" 
Matthew4:21 Leaving there, Jesus found three other men sitting in a boat, mending their nets. Two were brothers, Jacob and John, and they were with their father, Zebedee. Jesus called Jacob and John to his side and said to them, “Come and follow me.”" 
Matthew4:22 And at once they left their boat and their father, and began to follow Jesus." 
Matthew4:23 Jesus ministered from place to place throughout all of the province of Galilee. He taught in the synagogues, preaching the wonderful news of the kingdom and healing every kind of sickness and disease among the people." 
Matthew4:24 His fame spread throughout all Syria! Many people who were in pain and suffering with every kind of illness were brought to Jesus for their healing—epileptics, paralytics, and those tormented by demonic powers were all set free. Everyone who was brought to Jesus was healed!" 
Matthew4:25 This resulted in massive crowds of people following him, including people from Galilee, Jerusalem, the land of Judah, the region of the Ten Cities known as the Decapolis, and beyond the Jordan River." 
Matthew5:1 One day Jesus saw a vast crowd of people gathering to hear him, so he went up the slope of a hill and sat down. With his followers and disciples spread over the hillside," 
Matthew5:2 Jesus began to teach them: 
Matthew5:3 “What happiness comes to you when you feel your spiritual poverty! For yours is the realm of heaven’s kingdom. 
Matthew5:4 “What delight comes to you when you wait upon the Lord! For you will find what you long for. 
Matthew5:5 “What blessing comes to you when gentleness lives in you! For you will inherit the earth. 
Matthew5:6 “How enriched you are when you crave righteousness! For you will be satisfied. 
Matthew5:7 “How blessed you are when you demonstrate tender mercy! For tender mercy will be demonstrated to you. 
Matthew5:8 “What bliss you experience when your heart is pure! For then your eyes will open to see more and more of God. 
Matthew5:9 “How joyful you are when you make peace! For then you will be recognized as a true child of God. 
Matthew5:10 “How enriched you are when persecuted for doing what is right! For then you experience the realm of heaven’s kingdom. 
Matthew5:11 “How blessed you are when people insult and persecute you and speak all kinds of cruel lies about you because of your love for me! 
Matthew5:12 So leap for joy—since your heavenly reward is great. For you are being rejected the same way the prophets were before you. 
Matthew5:13 “Your lives are like salt among the people. But if you, like salt, become bland, how can your ‘saltiness’ be restored? Flavorless salt is good for nothing and will be thrown out and trampled on by others." 
Matthew5:14 “Your lives light up the world. For how can you hide a city that stands on a hilltop? 
Matthew5:15 And who would light a lamp and then hide it in an obscure place? Instead, it’s placed where everyone in the house can benefit from its light." 
Matthew5:16 So don’t hide your light! Let it shine brightly before others, so that your commendable works will shine as light upon them, and then they will give their praise to your Father in heaven.”" 
Matthew5:17 “If you think I’ve come to set aside the law of Moses or the writings of the prophets, you’re mistaken. I have come to bring to perfection all that has been written." 
Matthew5:18 Indeed, I assure you, as long as heaven and earth endure, not even the smallest detail of the Law will be done away with until its purpose is complete." 
Matthew5:19 So whoever violates even the least important of the commandments, and teaches others to do so, will be called least in heaven’s kingdom. But whoever obeys them and teaches their truths to others will be called great in heaven’s kingdom." 
Matthew5:20 For I tell you, unless your lives are more pure and full of integrity than the religious scholars and the Pharisees, you will never enter heaven’s kingdom.”" 
Matthew5:21 “You’re familiar with the commandment taught to those of old: ‘Do not murder or you will be judged.’ 
Matthew5:22 But I’m telling you, if you hold anger in your heart toward a fellow believer, you are subject to judgment. And whoever demeans and insults a fellow believer is answerable to the congregation. And whoever calls down curses upon a fellow believer is in danger of being sent to a fiery hell." 
Matthew5:23 “So then, if you are presenting a gift before the altar and suddenly you remember a quarrel you have with a fellow believer," 
Matthew5:24 leave your gift there in front of the altar and go at once to apologize to the one who is offended. Then, after you have reconciled, come to the altar and present your gift." 
Matthew5:25 It is always better to come to terms with the one who wants to sue you before you go to trial, or you may be found guilty by the judge, and he will hand you over to the officers, who will throw you into prison." 
Matthew5:26 Believe me, you won’t get out of prison until you have paid the full amount!”" 
Matthew5:27 “Your ancestors have been taught, ‘Never commit adultery.’" 
Matthew5:28 However, I say to you, if you look with lust in your eyes at a woman who is not your wife, you’ve already committed adultery in your heart." 
Matthew5:29 If your right eye seduces you to fall into sin, then go blind in your right eye! For you’re better off losing sight in one eye than to have your whole body thrown into hell." 
Matthew5:30 And if your right hand entices you to sin, let it go limp and useless! For you’re better off losing a part of your body than to have it all thrown into hell." 
Matthew5:31 “It has been said, ‘Whoever divorces his wife must give her legal divorce papers.’" 
Matthew5:32 However, I say to you, if anyone divorces his wife for any reason, except for infidelity, he causes her to commit adultery, and whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.”" 
Matthew5:33 “Again, your ancestors were taught, ‘Never swear an oath that you don’t intend to keep, but keep your vows to the Lord God.’" 
Matthew5:34 However, I say to you, don’t bind yourself by taking an oath at all. Don’t swear by heaven, for heaven is where God’s throne is placed." 
Matthew5:35 Don’t swear an oath by the earth, because it is the rug under God’s feet, and not by Jerusalem, because it is the city of the Great King." 
Matthew5:36 And why would you swear by your own head, because it’s not in your power to turn a single hair white or black? But just let your words ring true." 
Matthew5:37 A simple ‘Yes’ or ‘No’ will suffice. Anything beyond this springs from a deceiver. 
Matthew5:38 “Your ancestors have also been taught, ‘Take an eye in exchange for an eye and a tooth in exchange for a tooth.’" 
Matthew5:39 However, I say to you, don’t repay an evil act with another evil act. But whoever insults you by slapping you on the right cheek, turn the other to him as well." 
Matthew5:40 If someone is determined to sue you for your coat, give him the shirt off your back as a gift in return." 
Matthew5:41 And should people in authority take advantage of you, do more than what they demand." 
Matthew5:42 Learn to generously share what you have with those who ask for help, and don’t close your heart to the one who comes to borrow from you.”" 
Matthew5:43 “Your ancestors have also been taught ‘Love your neighbors and hate the one who hates you.’ 
Matthew5:44 However, I say to you, love your enemy, bless the one who curses you, do something wonderful for the one who hates you, and respond to the very ones who persecute you by praying for them." 
Matthew5:45 For that will reveal your identity as children of your heavenly Father. He is kind to all by bringing the sunrise to warm and rainfall to refresh whether a person does what is good or evil. 
Matthew5:46 What reward do you deserve if you only love the loveable? Don’t even the tax collectors do that? 
Matthew5:47 How are you any different from others if you limit your kindness only to your friends? Don’t even the ungodly do that? 
Matthew5:48 Since you are children of a perfect Father in heaven, become perfect like him.”" 
Matthew6:1 “Examine your motives to make sure you’re not showing off when you do your good deeds, only to be admired by others; otherwise, you will lose the reward of your heavenly Father." 
Matthew6:2 So when you give to the poor, don’t announce it and make a show of it just to be seen by people, like the hypocrites in the streets and in the marketplace. They’ve already received their reward!" 
Matthew6:3 But when you demonstrate generosity, do it with pure motives and without drawing attention to yourself." 
Matthew6:4 Give secretly and your Father, who sees all you do, will reward you openly.”" 
Matthew6:5 “Whenever you pray, be sincere and not like the pretenders who love the attention they receive while praying before others in the meetings and on street corners. Believe me, they’ve already received their reward." 
Matthew6:6 But whenever you pray, go into your innermost chamber and be alone with Father God, praying to him in secret. And your Father, who sees all you do, will reward you openly." 
Matthew6:7 When you pray, there is no need to repeat empty phrases, praying like the Gentiles do, for they expect God to hear them because of their many words." 
Matthew6:8 There is no need to imitate them, since your Father already knows what you need before you ask him." 
Matthew6:9 Pray like this: ‘Our Beloved Father, dwelling in the heavenly realms, may the glory of your name be the center on which our lives turn." 
Matthew6:10 Manifest your kingdom realm, and cause your every purpose to be fulfilled on earth, just as it is in heaven." 
Matthew6:11 We acknowledge you as our Provider of all we need each day. 
Matthew6:12 Forgive us the wrongs we have done as we ourselves release forgiveness to those who have wronged us. 
Matthew6:13 Rescue us every time we face tribulation and set us free from evil. For you are the King who rules with power and glory forever. Amen.’ 
Matthew6:14 “ And when you pray, make sure you forgive the faults of others so that your Father in heaven will also forgive you." 
Matthew6:15 But if you withhold forgiveness from others, your Father withholds forgiveness from you.”" 
Matthew6:16 “When you fast, don’t look gloomy and pretend to be spiritual. They want everyone to know they’re fasting, so they appear in public looking miserable and disheveled. Believe me, they’ve already received their reward." 
Matthew6:17 When you fast, don’t let it be obvious, but instead, wash your face and groom yourself and realize that your Father in the secret place is the one who is watching all that you do in secret and will continue to reward you.”" 
Matthew6:18  
Matthew6:19 “Don’t keep hoarding for yourselves earthly treasures that can be stolen by thieves. Material wealth eventually rusts, decays, and loses its value." 
Matthew6:20 Instead, stockpile heavenly treasures for yourselves that cannot be stolen and will never rust, decay, or lose their value." 
Matthew6:21 For your heart will always pursue what you esteem as your treasure. 
Matthew6:22 “The eyes of your spirit allow revelation-light to enter into your being. If your heart is unclouded, the light floods in!" 
Matthew6:23 But if your eyes are focused on money, the light cannot penetrate and darkness takes its place. How profound will be the darkness within you if the light of truth cannot enter!" 
Matthew6:24 “How could you worship two gods at the same time? You will have to hate one and love the other, or be devoted to one and despise the other. You can’t worship the true God while enslaved to the god of money!”" 
Matthew6:25 “This is why I tell you to never be worried about your life, for all that you need will be provided, such as food, water, clothing—everything your body needs. Isn’t there more to your life than a meal? Isn’t your body more than clothing?" 
Matthew6:26 “Consider the birds—do you think they worry about their existence? They don’t plant or reap or store up food, yet your heavenly Father provides them each with food. Aren’t you much more valuable to your Father than they?" 
Matthew6:27 So, which one of you by worrying could add anything to your life?" 
Matthew6:28 “And why would you worry about your clothing? Look at all the beautiful flowers of the field. They don’t work or toil," 
Matthew6:29 and yet not even Solomon in all his splendor was robed in beauty like one of these! 
Matthew6:30 So if God has clothed the meadow with hay, which is here for such a short time and then dried up and burned, won’t he provide for you the clothes you need—you of little faith?" 
Matthew6:31 “So then, forsake your worries! Why would you say, ‘What will we eat?’ or ‘What will we drink?’ or ‘What will we wear?’" 
Matthew6:32 For that is what the unbelievers chase after. Doesn’t your heavenly Father already know the things your bodies require? 
Matthew6:33 “So above all, constantly seek God’s kingdom and his righteousness, then all these less important things will be given to you abundantly." 
Matthew6:34 Refuse to worry about tomorrow, but deal with each challenge that comes your way, one day at a time. Tomorrow will take care of itself.”" 
Matthew7:1 “Refuse to be a critic full of bias toward others, and you will not be judged." 
Matthew7:2 For you’ll be judged by the same standard that you’ve used to judge others. The measurement you use on them will be used on you. 
Matthew7:3 Why would you focus on the flaw in someone else’s life and fail to notice the glaring flaws of your own? 
Matthew7:4 How could you say to your friend, ‘Let me show you where you’re wrong,’ when you’re guilty of even more?" 
Matthew7:5 You’re being hypercritical and a hypocrite! First acknowledge and deal with your own ‘blind spots,’ and then you’ll be capable of dealing with the ‘blind spot’ of your friend." 
Matthew7:6 “Who would hang earrings on a dog’s ear or throw pearls in front of wild pigs? They’ll only trample them under their feet and then turn around and tear you to pieces! 
Matthew7:7 “Ask, and the gift is yours. Seek, and you’ll discover. Knock, and the door will be opened for you." 
Matthew7:8 For every persistent one will get what he asks for. Every persistent seeker will discover what he longs for. And everyone who knocks persistently will one day find an open door. 
Matthew7:9 “Do you know of any parent who would give his hungry child, who asked for food, a plate of rocks instead?" 
Matthew7:10 Or when asked for a piece of fish, what parent would offer his child a snake instead?" 
Matthew7:11 If you, imperfect as you are, know how to lovingly take care of your children and give them what’s best, how much more ready is your heavenly Father to give wonderful gifts to those who ask him?”" 
Matthew7:12 “In everything you do, be careful to treat others in the same way you’d want them to treat you, for that is the essence of all the teachings of the Law and the Prophets." 
Matthew7:13 Enter through the narrow gate because the wide gate and broad path is the way that leads to destruction—nearly everyone chooses that crowded road! 
Matthew7:14 The narrow gate and the difficult way leads to eternal life—so few even find it!” 
Matthew7:15 “Constantly be on your guard against phony prophets. They come disguised as lambs, appearing to be genuine, but on the inside they are like wild, ravenous wolves!" 
Matthew7:16 You can spot them by their actions, for the fruits of their character will be obvious. You won’t find sweet grapes hanging on a thorn bush, and you’ll never pick good fruit from a tumbleweed." 
Matthew7:17 So if the tree is good, it will produce good fruit; but if the tree is bad, it will bear only rotten fruit and deserves to be cut down and burned." 
Matthew7:18  
Matthew7:19  
Matthew7:20 You’ll know them by the obvious fruit of their lives and ministries.” 
Matthew7:21 “Not everyone who says to me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ will enter into heaven’s kingdom. It is only those who persist in doing the will of my heavenly Father." 
Matthew7:22 On the day of judgment many will say to me, ‘Lord, Lord, don’t you remember us? Didn’t we prophesy in your name? Didn’t we cast out demons and do many miracles in your name?’" 
Matthew7:23 But I will have to say to them, ‘Go away from me, you lawless rebels! I’ve never been joined to you!’" 
Matthew7:24 “Everyone who hears my teaching and applies it to his life can be compared to a wise man who built his house on an unshakable foundation. 
Matthew7:25 When the rains fell and the flood came, with fierce winds beating upon his house, it stood firm because of its strong foundation." 
Matthew7:26 “But everyone who hears my teaching and does not apply it to his life can be compared to a foolish man who built his house on sand. 
Matthew7:27 When it rained and rained and the flood came, with wind and waves beating upon his house, it collapsed and was swept away.”" 
Matthew7:28 By the time Jesus finished speaking, the crowds were awestruck by his teaching," 
Matthew7:29 because his words carried such great authority, quite unlike the religious scholars." 
Matthew8:1 After he came down from teaching on the hillside, massive crowds began following him." 
Matthew8:2 Suddenly, a leper walked up to Jesus and threw himself down before him in worship and said, “Lord, you have the power to heal me . . . if you really want to.”" 
Matthew8:3 Jesus reached out his hand and touched the leper and said, “Of course I want to heal you—be healed!” And instantly, all signs of leprosy disappeared!" 
Matthew8:4 Then Jesus said to him, “Don’t speak to anyone, but go at once and find a priest and show him what has happened to you. Make sure to take the offering Moses commanded so he can certify your healing.”" 
Matthew8:5 When Jesus entered the village of Capernaum, a captain in the Roman army approached him, asking for a miracle." 
Matthew8:6 “Lord,” he said, “I have a son who is lying in my home, paralyzed and suffering terribly.”" 
Matthew8:7 Jesus responded, “I will go with you and heal him.”" 
Matthew8:8 But the Roman officer interjected, “Lord, who am I to have you come into my house? I understand your authority, for I too am a man who walks under authority and have authority over soldiers who serve under me. I can tell one to go and he’ll go, and another to come and he’ll come. I command my servants and they’ll do whatever I ask. So I know that all you need to do is to stand here and command healing over my son and he will be instantly healed.”" 
Matthew8:9  
Matthew8:10 Jesus was astonished when he heard this and said to those who were following him, “He has greater faith than anyone I’ve encountered in Israel!" 
Matthew8:11 Listen to what I am about to tell you. Multitudes of Gentiles will stream from the east and the west, to enter into the heavenly kingdom with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob." 
Matthew8:12 But many Israelites, born to be heirs of the kingdom, will be turned away and banished into the darkness where there will be bitter weeping and unbearable anguish.”" 
Matthew8:13 Then Jesus turned to the Roman officer and said, “Go home. All that you have believed for will be done for you!” And his son was healed at that very moment." 
Matthew8:14 Then Jesus entered Peter’s home and found Peter’s mother-in-law bedridden, severely ill with a fever." 
Matthew8:15 The moment Jesus touched her hand she was healed! Immediately she got up and began to make dinner for them. 
Matthew8:16 That evening the people brought to him many who were demonized. And by Jesus only speaking a word of healing over them, they were totally set free from their torment, and everyone who was sick received their healing!" 
Matthew8:17 In doing this, Jesus fulfilled the prophecy of Isaiah: He put upon himself our weaknesses, and he carried away our diseases and made us well." 
Matthew8:18 At the sight of large crowds gathering around him, Jesus gave orders to his disciples to get ready to sail back over to the other side of the lake." 
Matthew8:19 Just then, a religious scholar approached him and said, “Teacher, I’ll follow you wherever you go!”" 
Matthew8:20 Jesus replied, “Foxes have dens, birds have nests, but the Son of Man has no true home in this world.”" 
Matthew8:21 Then another man spoke up and said, “Lord, I’ll follow you, but first I must take care of my aged father and bury him when he dies.”" 
Matthew8:22 But Jesus said to him, “Now is the time to follow me, and let those who are dead bury their own dead.”" 
Matthew8:23 They all got into a boat and began to cross over to the other side of the lake. And Jesus, exhausted, fell asleep." 
Matthew8:24 Suddenly a violent storm developed, with waves so high the boat was about to be swamped. Yet Jesus continued to sleep soundly." 
Matthew8:25 The disciples woke him up, saying, “Save us, Lord! We’re going to die!”" 
Matthew8:26 But Jesus reprimanded them. “Why are you gripped with fear? Where is your faith?” Then he stood and rebuked the storm, saying, “Be still!” And instantly it became perfectly calm." 
Matthew8:27 The disciples were astonished by this miracle and said to one another, “Who is this Man? Even the wind and waves obey his Word.”" 
Matthew8:28 When they arrived on the other side of the lake, in the region of the Gadarenes, two demonized men confronted Jesus. They lived among the tombs of a cemetery and were considered so extremely violent that no one felt safe passing through that area." 
Matthew8:29 The demons screamed at Jesus, shouting, “Son of God, what do you want with us? Leave us alone! Have you come to torment us before the appointed time?”" 
Matthew8:30 There was a large herd of pigs feeding nearby. 
Matthew8:31 And the demons pleaded, “If you cast us out, send us into that herd of pigs.”" 
Matthew8:32 Jesus commanded, “Then go!” And at once the demons came out of the men and went into the pigs. Then the entire herd of crazed pigs stampeded down the steep slope and fell into the water and drowned." 
Matthew8:33 The men who were herding the pigs fled to the nearby town and informed the people of all that had happened to the demonized men. 
Matthew8:34 Then everyone from the town went out to confront Jesus and urged him to go away and leave them alone. 
Matthew9:1 Jesus got into the boat and returned to what was considered his hometown, Capernaum." 
Matthew9:2 Just then some people brought a paraplegic man to him, lying on a sleeping mat. When Jesus perceived the strong faith within their hearts, he said to the paralyzed man, “My son, be encouraged, for your sins have been forgiven.”" 
Matthew9:3 These words prompted some of the religious scholars to think, “Why, that’s nothing but blasphemy!”" 
Matthew9:4 Jesus supernaturally perceived their thoughts, and said to them, “Why do you carry such evil in your hearts?" 
Matthew9:5 Which is easier to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or, ‘Stand up and walk!’?" 
Matthew9:6 But now, to convince you that the Son of Man has been given authority to forgive sins, I say to this man, ‘Stand up, pick up your mat, and walk home.’ ”" 
Matthew9:7 Immediately the man sprang to his feet and left for home. 
Matthew9:8 When the crowds witnessed this miracle, they were awestruck. They shouted praises to God because he had given such authority to human beings." 
Matthew9:9 As Jesus left Capernaum he came upon a tax-collecting station, where a Jewish man named Matthew was collecting taxes for the Romans. “Come, follow me,” Jesus said to him. Immediately Matthew jumped up and began to follow Jesus." 
Matthew9:9 As Jesus left Capernaum he came upon a tax-collecting station, where a Jewish man named Matthew was collecting taxes for the Romans. “Come, follow me,” Jesus said to him. Immediately Matthew jumped up and began to follow Jesus." 
Matthew9:10 Jesus went to Matthew’s house and made himself at home. Many other tax collectors and outcasts of society were invited to eat with Jesus and his disciples. 
Matthew9:11 When those known as the Pharisees saw what was happening, they were indignant, and they kept asking Jesus’ disciples, “Why would your Master dine with such lowlifes?”" 
Matthew9:12 When Jesus overheard this, he spoke up and said, “Healthy people don’t need to see a doctor, but the sick will go for treatment.”" 
Matthew9:13 Then he added, “Now you should go and study the meaning of the verse: I want you to show mercy, not just offer me a sacrifice. For I have come to invite the outcasts of society and sinners, not those who think they are already on the right path.”" 
Matthew9:14 The disciples of John the Baptizer approached Jesus with this question: “Why is it that we and the Pharisees fast regularly, but not your disciples?”" 
Matthew9:14 The disciples of John the Baptizer approached Jesus with this question: “Why is it that we and the Pharisees fast regularly, but not your disciples?”" 
Matthew9:15 Jesus replied, “How can the sons of the bridal chamber grieve when the Bridegroom is next to them? But the days of fasting will come when the Bridegroom is taken away from them." 
Matthew9:16 And who would mend worn-out clothing with new fabric? When the new cloth shrinks it will rip, making the hole worse than before." 
Matthew9:17 And who would pour fresh, new wine into an old wineskin? Eventually the wine will ferment and make the wineskin burst, losing everything—the wine is spilled and the wineskin ruined. Instead, new wine is always poured into a new wineskin so that both are preserved.”" 
Matthew9:18 While Jesus was still speaking, an influential Jewish leader approached and knelt before him, saying, “Help me! My daughter has just died. Please come and place your hand upon her so that she will live again.”" 
Matthew9:19 So Jesus and his disciples got up and went with him. 
Matthew9:20 Suddenly, a woman came from behind Jesus and touched the tassel of his prayer shawl for healing. She had been suffering from continual bleeding for twelve years, but had faith that Jesus could heal her." 
Matthew9:21 For she kept saying to herself, “If I could only touch his prayer shawl I would be healed.”" 
Matthew9:22 Just then Jesus turned around and looked at her and said, “My daughter, be encouraged. Your faith has healed you.” And instantly she was healed!" 
Matthew9:23 When Jesus finally entered the home of the Jewish leader, he saw a noisy crowd of mourners, wailing and playing a funeral dirge on their flutes." 
Matthew9:24 He told them, “You must leave, for the little girl is not dead; she’s only asleep.” Then everyone began to ridicule him." 
Matthew9:25 After he made the crowd go outside, he went into the girl’s room and gently took hold of her hand. She immediately stood to her feet!" 
Matthew9:26 And the news of this incredible miracle spread everywhere. 
Matthew9:27 As Jesus left the house, two blind men began following him, shouting out over and over, “Son of David, show us mercy and heal us!”" 
Matthew9:28 And they followed him right into the house where Jesus was staying. So Jesus asked them, “Do you believe that I have the power to restore sight to your eyes?” They replied, “Yes Lord, we believe!”" 
Matthew9:29 Then Jesus put his hands over their eyes and said, “You will have what your faith expects!”" 
Matthew9:30 And instantly their eyes opened—they could see! Then Jesus warned them sternly, “Make sure that you tell no one what just happened!”" 
Matthew9:31 But unable to contain themselves, they went out and spread the news everywhere!" 
Matthew9:32 While they were leaving, some people brought before Jesus a demonized man who couldn’t speak." 
Matthew9:33 Jesus cast the demon out of him, and immediately the man began to speak plainly. The crowds marveled in astonishment, saying, “We’ve never seen miracles like this in Israel!”" 
Matthew9:34 But the Pharisees kept saying, “The chief of demons is helping him drive out demons.”" 
Matthew9:35 Jesus walked throughout the region with the joyful message of God’s kingdom realm. He taught in their meeting houses, and wherever he went he demonstrated God’s power by healing every kind of disease and illness." 
Matthew9:36 When he saw the vast crowds of people, Jesus’ heart was deeply moved with compassion, because they seemed weary and helpless, like wandering sheep without a shepherd." 
Matthew9:37 He turned to his disciples and said, “The harvest is huge and ripe! But there are not enough harvesters to bring it all in." 
Matthew9:38 As you go, plead with the Owner of the Harvest to thrust out many more reapers to harvest his grain!”" 
Matthew10:1 Jesus gathered his twelve disciples and imparted to them authority to cast out demons and to heal every sickness and every disease. 
Matthew10:2 Now, these are the names of the twelve apostles: first, Simon, who is nicknamed Peter, and Andrew, his brother. And then Jacob and John, sons of Zebedee." 
Matthew10:2 Now, these are the names of the twelve apostles: first, Simon, who is nicknamed Peter, and Andrew, his brother. And then Jacob and John, sons of Zebedee." 
Matthew10:3 Next were Phillip and Bartholomew; then Thomas and Matthew, the tax collector; Jacob the son of Alphaeus; Thaddeus;" 
Matthew10:4 Simon, the former member of the Zealot party, and Judas the locksmith, who eventually betrayed Jesus." 
Matthew10:5 Jesus sent out the Twelve with these instructions: “Don’t go into any Gentile or Samaritan territory. 
Matthew10:6 Go instead and find the lost sheep among the people of Israel. 
Matthew10:7 And as you go, preach this message: ‘Heaven’s kingdom realm is accessible, close enough to touch.’" 
Matthew10:8 You must continually bring healing to lepers and to those who are sick, and make it your habit to break off the demonic presence from people, and raise the dead back to life. Freely you have received the power of the kingdom, so freely release it to others." 
Matthew10:9 You won’t need a lot of money. 
Matthew10:10 Travel light, and don’t even pack an extra change of clothes in your backpack. Trust God for everything, because the one who works for him deserves to be provided for." 
Matthew10:11 “Whatever village or town you enter, search for an honorable man who will let you into his home until you leave for the next town." 
Matthew10:12 Once you enter a house, speak to the family there and say, ‘God’s blessing of peace be upon this house!’" 
Matthew10:13 And if those living there welcome you, let your peace come upon the house. But if you are rejected, that blessing of peace will come back upon you." 
Matthew10:14 And if anyone doesn’t listen to you and rejects your message, when you leave that house or town, shake the dust off your feet." 
Matthew10:15 Mark my words, on the day of judgment the wicked people who lived in the land of Sodom and Gomorrah will have a lesser degree of judgment than the city that rejects you, for the people of Sodom and Gomorrah did not have the opportunity that was given to them!" 
Matthew10:15 Mark my words, on the day of judgment the wicked people who lived in the land of Sodom and Gomorrah will have a lesser degree of judgment than the city that rejects you, for the people of Sodom and Gomorrah did not have the opportunity that was given to them!" 
Matthew10:16 Now, remember, it is I who sends you out, even though you feel vulnerable as lambs going into a pack of wolves. So be as shrewd as snakes yet as harmless as doves.”" 
Matthew10:17 “Be on your guard! For there will be those who will betray you before their religious councils and brutally beat you with whips in their public gatherings. 
Matthew10:18 And because you follow me, they will take you to stand trial in front of rulers and even kings as an opportunity to testify of me before them and the unbelievers." 
Matthew10:19 So when they arrest you, don’t worry about how to speak or what you are to say, for the Holy Spirit will give you at that very moment the words to speak." 
Matthew10:20 It won’t be you speaking but the Spirit of your Father speaking through you. 
Matthew10:21 “A brother will betray his brother unto death—even a father his child! Children will rise up against their parents and have them put to death. 
Matthew10:22 Expect to be hated by all because of my name, but be faithful to the end and you will experience life and deliverance." 
Matthew10:23 And when they persecute you in one town, flee to another. But I promise you this: you will not deliver all the cities and towns of Israel before the Son of Man comes." 
Matthew10:24 “A student is not superior to his teacher any more than a servant would be greater than his master. 
Matthew10:25 The student must be satisfied to share his teacher’s fate and the servant his master’s. If they have called the head of the family ‘lord of flies,’ no wonder they malign the members of his family." 
Matthew10:26 “Don’t be afraid or intimidated by others, for God will bring everything out into the open and every secret will be told." 
Matthew10:27 What I say to you in the dark, repeat in broad daylight, and what you hear in a whisper, announce it publicly." 
Matthew10:28 Don’t be in fear of those who can kill only the body but not your soul. Fear only God, who is able to destroy both soul and body in hell." 
Matthew10:29 You can buy two sparrows for only a copper coin, yet not even one sparrow falls from its nest without the knowledge of your Father. Aren’t you worth much more to God than many sparrows?" 
Matthew10:30 So don’t worry. For your Father cares deeply about even the smallest detail of your life. 
Matthew10:31  
Matthew10:32 “If you openly and publicly acknowledge me, I will freely and openly acknowledge you before my heavenly Father." 
Matthew10:33 But if you publicly deny that you know me, I will also deny you before my heavenly Father." 
Matthew10:34 “Perhaps you think I’ve come to spread peace and calm over the earth—but my coming will bring conflict and division, not peace." 
Matthew10:35 Because of me, A son will turn against his father, a daughter her mother and against her mother-in-law." 
Matthew10:36 Within your own families you will find enemies. 
Matthew10:37 “Whoever loves father or mother or son or daughter more than me is not fit to be my disciple. 
Matthew10:38 And whoever comes to me must follow in my steps and be willing to share my cross and experience it as his own, or he is not worthy of me." 
Matthew10:39 Those who cling to their lives will give up true life. But those who let go of their lives for my sake and surrender it all to me will discover true life! 
Matthew10:40 “Whoever receives you receives me, and whoever receives me receives the One who sent me." 
Matthew10:41 Whoever receives a prophet because he is God’s messenger will share a prophet’s reward. And whoever welcomes a righteous person because he follows me will also share in his reward. 
Matthew10:42 And whoever gives a cup of cold water to one of my disciples, I promise you, he will not go unrewarded.”"

Matthew11:6 And tell John that the blessing of heaven comes to those who are not offended over me.”

Matthew11:9 Or did you encounter a true prophet out in the lonely wilderness? Yes, John was a prophet like those of the past, but he is even more than that!"

Matthew11:11 “For I tell you the truth, throughout history there has never been a man who surpasses John the Baptizer. Yet the least of those who now experience heaven’s kingdom will become even greater than he."

Matthew11:15 So listen and understand what I’m telling you.

Matthew11:17 ‘You don’t like it when we want to play Wedding! And you don’t like it when we want to play Funeral! You will neither dance nor mourn.’ 
Matthew11:18 Why is it that when John came to you, neither feasting nor drinking wine, you said, ‘He has a demon in him!’?" 
Matthew11:18 Why is it that when John came to you, neither feasting nor drinking wine, you said, ‘He has a demon in him!’?" 
Matthew11:19 Yet when the Son of Man came and went to feasts and drank wine, you said, ‘Look at this man! He is nothing but a glutton and a drunkard! He spends all his time with tax collectors and other sinners.’ But God’s wisdom will become visible by those who embrace it.”" 
Matthew11:20 Then Jesus began to openly denounce the cities where he had done most of his mighty miracles, because the people failed to turn away from sin and return to God." 
Matthew11:21 He said, “How tragic it will be for the city of Korazin! And how horrible for the city of Bethsaida! For if the powerful miracles that I performed in Korazin and Bethsaida had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have humbled themselves and repented, and turned from their sins." 
Matthew11:22 Tyre and Sidon will be better off on judgment day than you! 
Matthew11:23 And Capernaum—do you really think you’ll be exalted because of the great miracles I have done there? No! You’ll be brought down to the depths of hell because of your rejection of me. For if the miracles I worked in your streets were done in Sodom, it would still be standing today!" 
Matthew11:24 But I tell you, it will be more bearable for the region of Sodom in the day of judgment than it will be for you.”" 
Matthew11:25 Then Jesus exclaimed, “Father, thank you, for you are Lord, the Supreme Ruler over heaven and earth! And you have hidden the great revelation of your authority from those who are proud and think they are wise and unveiled it instead to little children." 
Matthew11:26 Yes, Father, you’ve chosen this gracious plan to extend your kingdom." 
Matthew11:27 You have entrusted me with all that you are and all that you have. No one fully and intimately knows the Son except the Father. And no one fully and intimately knows the Father except the Son. But the Son is able to unveil the Father to anyone he chooses. 
Matthew11:28 “Are you weary, carrying a heavy burden? Come to me. I will refresh your life, for I am your oasis." 
Matthew11:29 Simply join your life with mine. Learn my ways and you’ll discover that I’m gentle, humble, easy to please. You will find refreshment and rest in me." 
Matthew11:30 For all that I require of you will be pleasant and easy to bear.” 
Matthew12:1 One Saturday, on the day of rest, Jesus and his disciples were walking through a field of wheat. The disciples were hungry, so they plucked off some heads of grain and rubbed them in their hands to eat." 
Matthew12:2 But when some of the Pharisees saw what was happening, they said to him, “Look! Your disciples shouldn’t be harvesting grain on the Sabbath!”" 
Matthew12:3 Jesus responded, “Haven’t you ever read what King David and his men did when they were hungry?" 
Matthew12:4 They entered the house of God and ate the sacred bread of God’s presence, violating the law by eating bread that only the priests were allowed to eat." 
Matthew12:5 “And haven’t you read in the Torah that the priests violated the rules of the Sabbath by carrying out their duties in the temple on a Saturday, and yet they are without blame?" 
Matthew12:6 But I say to you, there is one here who is even greater than the temple." 
Matthew12:7 If only you could learn the meaning of the words ‘I want compassion more than a sacrifice,’ you wouldn’t be condemning my innocent disciples." 
Matthew12:8 For the Son of Man exercises his lordship over the Sabbath.” 
Matthew12:9 Then Jesus left them and went into the synagogue where 
Matthew12:10 he encountered a man who had an atrophied, paralyzed hand. The fault-finding Pharisees asked Jesus, “Is it permissible to perform a work of healing on the Sabbath?” They only asked him this question because they hoped to accuse him of breaking the Jewish laws." 
Matthew12:11 He answered them, “If any of you had a lamb that fell into a ditch on the Sabbath, wouldn’t you reach out your hand and lift it out?" 
Matthew12:12 Isn’t a man much more valuable than a lamb? So of course, it’s always proper to do miracles, even on the Sabbath.”" 
Matthew12:13 Then he turned to the man and said, “Hold out your hand!” And as he stretched it out, it was restored, exactly like the other." 
Matthew12:14 Immediately the Pharisees went out and started to scheme about how they would destroy him. 
Matthew12:15 Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he left by another way. Massive crowds followed him from there, and he healed all who were sick." 
Matthew12:16 However, he sternly warned them not to tell others or disclose his real identity," 
Matthew12:17 in order to fulfill the prophecy of Isaiah: 
Matthew12:18 Take a careful look at my servant, my chosen one. I love him dearly and I find all my delight in him. I will breathe my Spirit upon him and he will decree justice to the nations." 
Matthew12:19 He will not quarrel or raise his voice in public. 
Matthew12:20 He won’t brush aside the bruised and broken. He will be gentle with the weak and feeble, until his victory releases justice." 
Matthew12:21 And the fame of his name will birth hope among the people. 
Matthew12:22 Then a man was brought before Jesus who had a demon spirit that made him both blind and mute. Jesus healed him instantly, and he could see and talk again!" 
Matthew12:23 The crowds went wild with amazement as they witnessed this miracle. And they kept saying to one another, “Could this man be the Messiah?”" 
Matthew12:24 But when the Pharisees overheard what the people were saying, they said, “He casts out demons by the power of Satan, the prince of demons!”" 
Matthew12:25 Jesus supernaturally perceived their thoughts and motives, so he told them this parable: “Any kingdom that fights against itself is reduced to ruins. And any family or community splintered by strife will fall apart." 
Matthew12:26 So if Satan casts out Satan, he is making war on himself. How then could his kingdom stand?" 
Matthew12:27 So if Satan empowers me to cast out demons, who empowers your exorcists to cast them out? Go ask them, let them be your judges." 
Matthew12:28 But, if I drive out demons by the power of the Spirit of God, then the end of Satan’s kingdom has come!" 
Matthew12:29 Who would dare enter the house of a mighty man and steal his property? First he must be overpowered and tied up by one who is stronger than he. Then his entire house can be plundered. 
Matthew12:30 “ So join with me, for if you’re not on my side you are against me. And if you refuse to help me gather the spoils, you are making things worse." 
Matthew12:31 This is why I warn you. God will forgive people for every sin and blasphemy except one. There is no forgiveness for the sin of blasphemy against the Holy Spirit. 
Matthew12:32 If anyone speaks evil of me, the Son of Man, he can be forgiven; but if anyone speaks against the Holy Spirit, it will never be forgiven, now or ever!”" 
Matthew12:33 “You must determine if a tree is good or rotten. You can recognize good trees by their delicious fruit. But if you find rotten fruit, you can be certain that the tree is rotten. The fruit defines the tree." 
Matthew12:34 But you who are known as the Pharisees are rotten to the core like venomous snakes. How can your words be good if you are rotten within? For what has been stored up in your hearts will be heard in the overflow of your words! 
Matthew12:35 “When virtue is stored within, the hearts of good, upright people will produce good fruit. But when evil is hidden within, those who are evil will produce evil fruit." 
Matthew12:36 You can be sure of this: when the day of judgment comes, everyone will be held accountable for every careless word he has spoken." 
Matthew12:37 Your very words will be used as evidence, and your words will declare you either innocent or guilty.”" 
Matthew12:38 Then a few Jewish scholars and Pharisees spoke up and said, “Teacher, why don’t you perform a miraculous sign for us.”" 
Matthew12:39 Jesus replied, “Only evil people who are unfaithful to God would demand a sign. There will be no sign given to you except the sign of the prophet Jonah." 
Matthew12:40 For as Jonah was in the belly of the huge sea creature for three days and three nights, so the Son of Man will be in the heart of the earth for three days and three nights." 
Matthew12:41 The people of Nineveh will also rise up on the day of judgment to condemn this generation. For they all repented when they heard the preaching of Jonah. And there is one greater than Jonah here preaching to you today! 
Matthew12:42 Even the Queen of Sheba will rise up on the day of judgment to condemn this generation for its unbelief! She journeyed from a far and distant land just to hear the wisdom of King Solomon. Yet now there is one greater than Solomon speaking to you today.” 
Matthew12:43 “When a demon is cast out of a person, it roams around a dry region, looking for a place to rest, but never finds it." 
Matthew12:44 Then it says, ‘I’ll return to the house I moved out of,’ and so it goes back, only to find that the house is vacant, warm, and ready for it to move back in." 
Matthew12:45 So it goes looking for seven other demons more evil than itself, and they all enter together to live there. Then the person’s condition becomes much worse than it was in the beginning. This describes what will also happen to the people of this evil generation.”" 
Matthew12:46 While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers came and stood outside, asking for him to come out and speak with them." 
Matthew12:47 Then someone said, “Look, your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to have a word with you.”" 
Matthew12:48 But Jesus just looked at him and said, “Let me introduce you to my true mother and brothers.”" 
Matthew12:49 Then gesturing to the disciples gathered around him, he said, “Look closely, for this is my true family." 
Matthew12:50 When you obey my heavenly Father, that makes you a part of my true family.”" 
Matthew13:1 Later that day, Jesus left the house and sat by the lakeshore to teach the people." 
Matthew13:2 Soon, there were so many people surrounding him that he had to teach sitting in a boat while the large crowd stood on the shore." 
Matthew13:3 He taught them many things by using stories, parables to illustrate spiritual truths, saying: “Consider this: There was a farmer who went out to sow seeds." 
Matthew13:4 As he cast his seeds, some fell along the beaten path and the birds came and ate them." 
Matthew13:5 Others fell onto gravel that had no topsoil. They quickly shot up," 
Matthew13:6 but when the days grew hot, they were scorched and withered because they had insufficient roots." 
Matthew13:7 Others fell among the thorns, so when they sprouted, the thorns choked them." 
Matthew13:8 But other seeds fell on good, rich soil that kept producing a good harvest. Some yielded thirty, some sixty, and some even one hundred times as much as he planted!" 
Matthew13:9 If you’re able to understand this, then you need to respond.”" 
Matthew13:10 Then his disciples approached Jesus and asked, “Why do you always speak to people in these hard-to-understand parables?”" 
Matthew13:11 He explained, “You’ve been given the intimate experience of insight into the hidden mysteries of the realm of heaven’s kingdom, but they have not." 
Matthew13:12 For everyone who listens with an open heart will receive progressively more revelation until he has more than enough. But those who don’t listen with an open, teachable heart, even the understanding that they think they have will be taken from them." 
Matthew13:13 That’s why I teach the people using parables, because they think they’re looking for truth, yet because their hearts are unteachable, they never discover it. Although they will listen to me, they never fully perceive the message I speak." 
Matthew13:14 The prophecy of Isaiah describes them perfectly: Although they listen carefully to everything I speak, they don’t understand a thing I say. They look and pretend to see, but the eyes of their hearts are closed." 
Matthew13:15 Their minds are dull and slow to perceive, their ears are plugged and are hard of hearing, and they have deliberately shut their eyes to the truth. Otherwise they would open their eyes to see, and open their ears to hear, and open their minds to understand. Then they would turn to me and I would instantly heal them." 
Matthew13:16 “But blissful are your eyes, for they see. Delighted are your ears, for they are open to hear all these things." 
Matthew13:17 Many prophets and godly people yearned to see these days of miracles that you’ve been favored to see. They would have given everything to hear the revelation you’ve been favored to hear. 
Matthew13:18 “Now you are ready to hear the explanation of the parable of the sower: 
Matthew13:19 “What was sown along the path represents the one who listens to the message of the kingdom but doesn’t understand it. The Adversary then comes and snatches away what was sown into his heart. 
Matthew13:20 “The one sown on gravel represents the person who gladly hears the kingdom message, but his experience remains shallow." 
Matthew13:21 Shortly after he hears it, troubles and persecutions come because of the kingdom message he received. Then he quickly falls away, for the truth didn’t sink deeply into his heart." 
Matthew13:22 “The one sown among thorns represents one who receives the message, but all of life’s busy distractions, his divided heart, and his ambition for wealth result in suffocating the kingdom message and it becomes fruitless." 
Matthew13:23 “But what was sown on good, rich soil represents the one who hears and fully embraces the message of the kingdom. Their lives bear good fruit—some yield a harvest of thirty, sixty, even one hundred times as much as was sown.”" 
Matthew13:24 Then Jesus taught them another parable: “Heaven’s kingdom can be compared to a farmer who planted good seed in his field. 
Matthew13:25 But when everyone was asleep, an enemy came and planted weeds among the wheat and ran away." 
Matthew13:26 When the wheat sprouted and bore grain, the weeds also appeared." 
Matthew13:27 So the farmer’s hired hands came to him and said, ‘Sir, wasn’t that good seed that you sowed in the field? Where did all these weeds come from?’" 
Matthew13:28 “He answered, ‘This has to be the work of an enemy!’ “They replied, ‘Do you want us to go and gather up all the weeds?’" 
Matthew13:29 “ ‘No,’ he said. ‘If you pull out the weeds you might uproot the wheat at the same time." 
Matthew13:30 Let them both grow together until the harvest. At that time, I’ll tell my harvesters to gather the weeds first and tie them all in bundles to be burned. Then they will harvest the wheat and put it into my barn.’ ”" 
Matthew13:31 Then Jesus taught them another parable: “Heaven’s kingdom can be compared to the tiny mustard seed that a man takes and plants in his field. 
Matthew13:32 Although the smallest of all the seeds, it eventually grows into the greatest of garden plants, becoming a tree for birds to come and build their nests in its branches.”" 
Matthew13:33 Then he taught them another parable: “Heaven’s kingdom can be compared to yeast that a woman takes and blends into three measures of flour and then waits until all the dough rises.” 
Matthew13:34 Whenever Jesus addressed the crowds, he always spoke in allegories. He never spoke without using parables." 
Matthew13:35 He did this to fulfill the prophecy: I will speak to you in allegories. I will reveal secrets that have been concealed since before the foundation of the world. 
Matthew13:36 Jesus left the crowds and went inside the house where he was staying. Then his disciples approached him and asked, “Please explain the deeper meaning of the parable of the weeds growing in the field of wheat.”" 
Matthew13:37 He answered, “The man who sowed his field with good seed is the Son of Man." 
Matthew13:38 And the field is the world. The good seeds I sow are the children of the kingdom realm. The weeds are the children of the Evil One," 
Matthew13:39 and the enemy who sows them is the devil. The harvest points to the end of this age, and the harvesters are God’s messengers." 
Matthew13:40 As the weeds are bundled up and thrown into the fire, so it will be at the close of the age." 
Matthew13:41 The Son of Man will send his messengers, and they will uproot everything out of his kingdom. All the lawless ones and everything that causes sin will be removed." 
Matthew13:42 And they will throw them into the fiery furnace, where they will experience great sorrow and anguish." 
Matthew13:43 Then the righteous will shine like the brightness of the sun in their Father’s kingdom realm. If you’re able to understand this, then you’d better respond!”" 
Matthew13:44 “Heaven’s kingdom realm can be illustrated like this: “A person discovered that there was hidden treasure in a field. Upon finding it, he hid it again. Because of uncovering such treasure, he was overjoyed and sold all that he possessed to buy the entire field just so he could have the treasure." 
Matthew13:45 “Heaven’s kingdom realm is also like a jewel merchant in search of rare pearls. 
Matthew13:46 When he discovered one very precious and exquisite pearl, he immediately gave up all he had in exchange for it.”" 
Matthew13:47 “Again, heaven’s kingdom realm is like a fisherman who casts his large net into the lake, catching an assortment of fish." 
Matthew13:48 When the net was filled, the fishermen hauled it up on the shore, and they all sat down to sort out their catch. They collected the good in baskets and threw the bad away." 
Matthew13:49 And so it will be at the close of the age. The messengers will come and separate the evil from among the godly 
Matthew13:50 and throw them into the fiery furnace, where they will experience great sorrow and anguish." 
Matthew13:51 Now do you understand all this?” “Yes,” they replied." 
Matthew13:52 He responded, “Every scholar of the Scriptures, who is instructed in the ways of heaven’s kingdom realm, is like a wealthy home owner with his house filled with treasures both new and old.”" 
Matthew13:53 Right after Jesus taught this series of parables, he left from there." 
Matthew13:54 When Jesus arrived in his hometown of Nazareth, he began teaching the people in the synagogue. Everyone was dazed, overwhelmed with astonishment over the depth of revelation they were hearing. They said to one another, “Where did this man get such great wisdom and miraculous powers?" 
Matthew13:55 Isn’t he just the craftsman’s son? Isn’t his mother named Mary, and his four brothers Jacob, Joseph, Simon, and Judah?" 
Matthew13:56 And don’t his sisters all live here in Nazareth? From where then did he get all this revelation and power?” 
Matthew13:57 And the people became offended and began to turn against him. Jesus said, “There’s only one place a prophet isn’t honored—his own hometown!”" 
Matthew13:58 And their unbelief kept him from doing many mighty miracles in Nazareth. 
Matthew14:1 At that time Herod, the Roman ruler over Galilee, heard reports about Jesus." 
Matthew14:2 He told his officials, “This man has to be John the Baptizer who has come back from the dead. That’s why he has this power to work miracles.”" 
Matthew14:2 He told his officials, “This man has to be John the Baptizer who has come back from the dead. That’s why he has this power to work miracles.”" 
Matthew14:3 For Herod had earlier arrested John for confronting him for taking the wife of his brother Philip. He had John thrown in prison and placed in chains 
Matthew14:3 For Herod had earlier arrested John for confronting him for taking the wife of his brother Philip. He had John thrown in prison and placed in chains 
Matthew14:4 because John had repeatedly said to him, “It’s not legal or proper for you to be married to Herodias, your sister-in-law!”" 
Matthew14:4 because John had repeatedly said to him, “It’s not legal or proper for you to be married to Herodias, your sister-in-law!”" 
Matthew14:5 So Herod wanted John dead, but he was afraid of the crowds who flocked to John because they considered him to be a prophet." 
Matthew14:5 So Herod wanted John dead, but he was afraid of the crowds who flocked to John because they considered him to be a prophet." 
Matthew14:6 During Herod’s birthday celebration, the daughter of Herodias danced before Herod and all his distinguished guests, which greatly pleased the king." 
Matthew14:7 So he said to her in front of them all, “I give you my oath, ask of me anything you wish and it will be yours!”" 
Matthew14:8 Because she had been instructed by her mother, she said, “I want the head of John the Baptizer here on a platter!”" 
Matthew14:8 Because she had been instructed by her mother, she said, “I want the head of John the Baptizer here on a platter!”" 
Matthew14:9 This grieved the king, but because of his oath in front of all of his guests," 
Matthew14:10 he had John beheaded in prison. 
Matthew14:10 he had John beheaded in prison. 
Matthew14:11 They brought in his head and displayed it to her on a platter, and she then had it shown to her mother." 
Matthew14:12 John’s disciples went into the prison and carried his body away and buried it. Then they left to find Jesus and tell him what had happened. 
Matthew14:12 John’s disciples went into the prison and carried his body away and buried it. Then they left to find Jesus and tell him what had happened. 
Matthew14:13 On hearing this, Jesus slipped away privately by boat to be alone. But when the crowds discovered he had sailed away, they emerged from all the nearby towns and followed him on foot." 
Matthew14:14 So when Jesus landed he had a huge crowd waiting for him. Seeing so many people, his heart was deeply moved with compassion toward them, so he healed all the sick who were in the crowd." 
Matthew14:15 Later that afternoon the disciples came to Jesus and said, “It’s going to be dark soon and the people are hungry, but there’s nothing to eat here in this desolate place. You should send the crowds away to the nearby villages to buy themselves some food.”" 
Matthew14:16 “They don’t need to leave,” Jesus responded. “You can give them something to eat.”" 
Matthew14:17 They answered, “But all we have is five barley loaves and two fish.”" 
Matthew14:18 “Let me have them,” Jesus replied." 
Matthew14:19 Then he had everyone sit down on the grass and he then took the five loaves and two fish. He looked up into heaven, gave thanks to God, and broke the bread into pieces. He then gave it to his disciples, who in turn gave it to the crowds." 
Matthew14:20 And everyone ate until they were satisfied, for the food was multiplied in front of their eyes! They picked up the leftovers and filled up twelve baskets full!" 
Matthew14:21 There were about five thousand men who were fed, in addition to many women and children!" 
Matthew14:22 As soon as the people were fed, Jesus told his disciples to get into their boat and to go to the other side of the lake while he stayed behind to dismiss the people." 
Matthew14:23 After the crowds dispersed, Jesus went up into the hills to pray. And as night fell he was there praying alone." 
Matthew14:24 But the disciples, who were now in the middle of the lake, ran into trouble, for their boat was tossed about by the high winds and heavy seas." 
Matthew14:25 At about four o’clock in the morning, Jesus came to them, walking on the waves!" 
Matthew14:26 When the disciples saw him walking on top of the water, they were terrified and screamed, “A ghost!”" 
Matthew14:27 Then Jesus said, “Be brave and don’t be afraid. I am here!”" 
Matthew14:28 Peter shouted out, “Lord, if it’s really you, then have me join you on the water!”" 
Matthew14:29 “Come and join me,” Jesus replied. So Peter stepped out onto the water and began to walk toward Jesus." 
Matthew14:30 But when he realized how high the waves were, he became frightened and started to sink. “Save me, Lord!” he cried out." 
Matthew14:31 Jesus immediately stretched out his hand and lifted him up and said, “What little faith you have! Why would you let doubt win?”" 
Matthew14:32 And the very moment they both stepped into the boat, the raging wind ceased." 
Matthew14:33 Then all the disciples bowed down before him and worshiped Jesus. They said in adoration, “You are truly the Son of God!”" 
Matthew14:34 After they crossed over and landed at Gennesaret," 
Matthew14:35 the people living there quickly recognized who he was. They were quick to spread the news throughout the surrounding region that Jesus had come to them. 
Matthew14:36 So they brought him all their sick, begging him to let them touch the fringe of his cloak. And everyone who touched it was instantly healed!" 
Matthew15:1 Then the Pharisees and religious scholars came from Jerusalem and asked Jesus this question: 
Matthew15:2 “Why do your disciples ignore the traditions of our elders? For example, they don’t ceremonially wash their hands before they eat bread.”" 
Matthew15:3 Jesus answered, “And why do you ignore the commandment of God because of your traditions?" 
Matthew15:4 For didn’t God say, ‘Honor your father and mother,’ and, ‘Whoever abuses or insults his father or mother must be put to death?’" 
Matthew15:5 “But you teach that it’s permissible to say to your parents when they are in financial need, ‘Whatever gift you would have received from me I can keep for myself, since I dedicated it as an offering to God.’" 
Matthew15:6 This doesn’t honor your father or mother. And you have elevated your tradition above the words of God. 
Matthew15:7 Frauds and hypocrites! Isaiah described you perfectly when he said: 
Matthew15:8 These people honor me only with their words, for their hearts are so very distant from me." 
Matthew15:9 They pretend to worship me, but their worship is nothing more than the empty traditions of men.”" 
Matthew15:10 Then Jesus turned to the crowd and said, “Come, listen and open your heart to understand." 
Matthew15:11 What truly contaminates a person is not what he puts into his mouth but what comes out of his mouth. That’s what makes people defiled.” 
Matthew15:12 Then his disciples approached him and said, “Don’t you know that what you just said offended the Pharisees?”" 
Matthew15:13 Jesus replied, “Every plant that my heavenly Father didn’t plant is destined to be uprooted." 
Matthew15:14 Stay away from them, for they’re nothing more than blind guides. Do you know what happens when a blind man pretends to guide another blind man? They both stumble into a ditch!”" 
Matthew15:15 Peter spoke up and said, “Will you explain to us what you mean by your parable?”" 
Matthew15:16 Jesus said, “Even after all that I’ve taught you, you still remain clueless?" 
Matthew15:17 Is it hard to understand that whatever you eat enters the stomach only to pass out into the sewer? 
Matthew15:18 But what comes out of your mouth reveals the core of your heart. Words can pollute, not food." 
Matthew15:19 You will find living within an impure heart evil ideas, murderous thoughts, adultery, sexual immorality, theft, lies, and slander." 
Matthew15:20 That’s what pollutes a person. Eating with unwashed hands doesn’t defile anyone.” 
Matthew15:21 Then Jesus left and went north into the non-Jewish region of Lebanon. 
Matthew15:22 He encountered there a Canaanite woman who shouted out to him, “Lord, Son of David, show mercy to me! My daughter is horribly afflicted by a demon that torments her.”" 
Matthew15:23 But Jesus never answered her. So his disciples said to him, “Why do you ignore this woman who is crying out to us?”" 
Matthew15:24 Jesus said, “I’ve only been sent to the lost sheep of Israel.”" 
Matthew15:25 But she came and bowed down before him and said, “Lord, help me!”" 
Matthew15:26 Jesus responded, “It’s not right for a man to take bread from his children and throw it out to the dogs.”" 
Matthew15:27 “You’re right, Lord,” she replied. “But even puppies get to eat the crumbs that fall from the prince’s table.”" 
Matthew15:28 Then Jesus answered her, “Dear woman, your faith is strong! What you desire will be done for you.” And at that very moment, her daughter was instantly set free from demonic torment." 
Matthew15:29 After leaving Lebanon, Jesus went to Lake Galilee and climbed a hill nearby and sat down." 
Matthew15:30 Then huge crowds of people streamed up the hill, bringing with them the lame, blind, deformed, mute, and many others in need of healing. They laid them at Jesus’ feet and he healed them all." 
Matthew15:31 And the crowds marveled with amazement, astounded over the things they were witnessing with their own eyes! The lame were walking, the mute were speaking, the crippled were made well, and the blind could see. For three days everyone celebrated the miracles as they exalted and praised the God of Israel!" 
Matthew15:32 Jesus called his disciples to himself and said, “I care deeply about all these people, for they’ve already been with me for three days without food. I don’t want to send them away fasting or else they may be overcome by weakness on their journey home.”" 
Matthew15:33 The disciples said to him, “Where in the world are we going to find enough food in this desolate place to feed this crowd?”" 
Matthew15:34 “How many barley loaves do you have?” Jesus asked. “Seven,” they replied, “and a few small fish.”" 
Matthew15:35 So he gave the order, “Have the people sit down on the grass.”" 
Matthew15:36 Then he took the seven loaves and the fish and gave thanks to God. He broke the bread and gave it to his disciples, who then distributed the food to the crowds." 
Matthew15:37 When everyone was full and satisfied, they gathered up the leftovers. And from what was once seven loaves and a few fish, they filled seven baskets!" 
Matthew15:38 There were four thousand men who ate the food Jesus multiplied, and even more including the women and children!" 
Matthew15:39 After dismissing the crowd, Jesus got into the boat and crossed over to the region of Magdala."
